channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Trump says he wants to imprison US citizens in El Salvador. That's likely illegal
item.description: President Donald Trump on Monday reiterated that he'd like to send U.S. citizens who commit violent crimes to prison in El Salvador, telling that country's president, Nayib Bukele, that he'd “have to build five more places” to hold the potential new arrivals.
item.dc:creator: NICHOLAS RICCARDI, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Former Colorado deputy gets 3 years in prison for fatally shooting man who called for help
item.description: DENVER (AP) — A former Colorado sheriff’s deputy convicted of killing a 22-year-old man in distress who called 911 for help was sentenced Monday to three years in prison, the maximum sentence, by a judge who said the shooting was about power.
item.dc:creator: COLLEEN SLEVIN, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Trump administration freezes $2.2 billion in grants to Harvard over campus activism
item.description: The federal government says it’s freezing more than $2.2 billion in grants and $60 million in contracts to Harvard University, since the institution said Monday it won’t comply with the Trump administration’s demands to limit activism on campus.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Phone calls, police interview footage shared during Lori Vallow Daybell trial
item.description: PHOENIX (ABC4) -- The Idaho mother convicted of killing her two children returned to the courtroom Monday on charges of conspiracy to murder her husband, Charles Vallow. Lori Vallow Daybell is representing herself in Maricopa County during this trial.
item.dc:creator: Sorina Trauntvein, ABC 4

item.title: Video shows mother pick up baby and run during Southern California earthquake
item.description: A quick-thinking San Diego County mother saved her baby from a 5.2 magnitude earthquake in eastern San Diego County.
item.dc:creator: Sergio Robles, KRON4

item.title: Pennsylvania governor's mansion arson suspect Cody Balmer displays bizarre behavior before court arraignment
item.description: Cody Balmer, the suspect accused of setting fire to Pennsylvania Gov. Josh Shapiro's mansion over the weekend, appeared in court on Monday, where he stuck his tongue out at reporters.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Seven US service members had ‘COVID-19-like symptoms’ after 2019 Wuhan games: Pentagon report
item.description: Seven U.S. service members exhibited “COVID-19-like symptoms” during or after their return from the 2019 World Military Games in Wuhan, China, according to a Pentagon report recently made public.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Judge denies bail for man accused of setting fire at Pennsylvania Gov. Josh Shapiro's residence
item.description: HARRISBURG, Pa. (AP) — A man who allegedly scaled an iron security fence in the middle of the night, eluded police and set fire to the Pennsylvania governor’s mansion was denied bail Monday as he faced charges including attempted homicide, terrorism and arson.
item.dc:creator: MARK SCOLFORO and MARC LEVY, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Scholar spared prison in US case alleging China spies on dissidents abroad
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A Chinese American scholar convicted of spying on Chinese dissidents was spared prison time Monday by a U.S. judge. Shujun Wang was sentenced by U.S. Circuit Judge Denny Chin to time served and three years’ supervised release, according to attorneys.
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER PELTZ and MICHAEL HILL, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: El Salvador President Bukele says he won't be releasing a Maryland man back to the US
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump's top advisers and Nayib Bukele, the president of El Salvador, said Monday that they have no basis for the small Central American nation to return a Maryland man who was wrongly deported there last month.
item.dc:creator: SEUNG MIN KIM and MARCOS ALEMÁN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Massive North Carolina bluefin tuna pier fishing halted after wild run
item.description: "Not every day you see someone catch a bluefin tuna off the pier!!" said Dauses, of Newport News, Virginia.
item.dc:creator: Rodney Overton, NewsNation

item.title: FEMA denies Washington state disaster relief from bomb cyclone, governor says
item.description: The Federal Emergency Management Agency has denied Washington state's request for emergency relief funds to help repair an estimated $34 million in damage from a deadly bomb cyclone storm system in November, according to Gov. Bob Ferguson.
item.dc:creator: LISA BAUMANN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Self-driving Tesla hits car, sign while driver sleeps behind wheel
item.description: According to police, the driver was asleep at the wheel and using Tesla's self-driving feature to navigate.
item.dc:creator: Drew Kolek, NewsNation

item.title: AG: Man charged for AI-generated child sex material
item.description: “This proactive investigation uncovered a defendant weaponizing modern technology to victimize and harm children,” Attorney General said.
item.dc:creator: Hayden Thompson, NewsNation

item.title: Teen's parents to other sextortion victims: Ask for help
item.description: The family of a 14-year-old West Michigan boy who died by suicide after he was blackmailed over explicit pictures hopes sharing his story will save lives.
item.dc:creator: Susan Samples, NewsNation

item.title: A Palestinian activist expecting a US citizenship interview is arrested instead by ICE in Vermont
item.description: A Palestinian man who led protests against the war in Gaza as a student at Columbia University was arrested Monday at a Vermont immigration office where he expected to be interviewed about finalizing his U.S. citizenship, his attorneys said.
item.dc:creator: PATRICK WHITTLE and HOLLY RAMER, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: White House launches national security investigation into pharma, semiconductors
item.description: The Trump administration disclosed Monday that it had opened an investigation into the effects on national security of importing certain pharmaceuticals, a move widely seen as the prelude to initiating tariffs on prescription drugs.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Weixel, NewsNation

item.title: Diddy pleads not guilty to additional charges in new indictment
item.description: The music mogul faces federal charges of racketeering, sex trafficking and of transportation to engage in prostitution.
item.dc:creator: Safia Samee Ali, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Alleged member of Cuba's Ministry of the Interior arrested by ICE agents in Miami
item.description: U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement officials (ICE) arrested an alleged agent of Cuba’s Ministry of the Interior in Miami for unlawfully being in the country.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump sued over 'Liberation Day' tariffs
item.description: President Trump’s “Liberation Day” tariffs came under their first major legal challenge Monday, brought by a libertarian public-interest firm that argues the president overstepped his authority.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, NewsNation

item.title: Sheryl Crow's Tesla sold at auction in Indiana
item.description: Ray Wayne's Auto Group from Russellville, Kentucky, purchased the Grammy-winning artist's car for around $24,000.
item.dc:creator: Isaiah Calalang, NewsNation

item.title: Justice Department charges man with arson at New Mexico Tesla dealership and GOP headquarters
item.description: The Justice Department has charged a man with vandalizing a Tesla dealership in New Mexico and setting fire to the headquarters of the state Republican Party.
item.dc:creator: Alanna Durkin Richer And Eric Tucker, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Reported intruder arrested near UnitedHealthcare headquarters months after CEO's killing
item.description: Police in the Minneapolis suburb of Minnetonka said the suspect was arrested outside of the United Healthcare corporate campus.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Harvard rejects Trump demands for funding
item.description: Harvard University on Monday rejected demands from the Trump administration as it threatens the school's federal funding as part of a broader clampdown on higher education.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, NewsNation

item.title: Trump says he's open to sending violent criminals who are US citizens to El Salvador prison
item.description: President Trump on Monday indicated he would be open to sending American citizens who are violent criminals to El Salvador to be held in a notorious prison there, telling reporters his administration is studying law on the matter.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: Nvidia plans to manufacture AI chips in the US for the first time
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Nvidia announced Monday that it will produce its artificial intelligence supercomputers in the United States for the first time.
item.dc:creator: SARAH PARVINI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: One US college student released after two detained in Denmark for alleged Uber incident
item.description: One of the two U.S. college students detained in Denmark over an alleged assault following an Uber ride has been released, according to a statement from his family.
item.dc:creator: Carlos Mathis, WFLA, NBC

item.title: The dead in upstate New York plane crash included 2022 NCAA woman of the year and family members
item.description: A private plane that crashed in upstate New York over the weekend was carrying six members of a close-knit family of physicians and distinguished student-athletes on a trip to the Catskills for a birthday celebration and the Passover holiday.
item.dc:creator: JAKE OFFENHARTZ, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Grand jury indicts sailor for animal torture after dog starves to death
item.description: VIRGINIA BEACH, Va. (WAVY) — A grand jury has indicted a sailor for animal torture, one year after a dog starved to death in Virginia Beach. When Enonge Stovall was getting deployed, she said the last thing on her mind coming back was that her dog would be dead.
item.dc:creator: Keagan Hughes, NewsNation

item.title: Trump pharma tariff threat raises specter of shortages, price hikes
item.description: The pharmaceutical industry is bracing for chaos if President Trump follows through on his threat to impose "a major tariff" on prescription drug imports.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Weixel, NewsNation

item.title: Suspect arsonist was prepared to attack Pennsylvania Gov. Shapiro with hammer, court docs reveal
item.description: Court documents concerning Cody Balmer, the man charged in connection with a fire at Pennsylvania Gov. Josh Shapiro's residence, have revealed details of the suspect's alleged plot.
item.dc:creator: Brady Doran, WGN 9

item.title: NYC helicopter tour company shuts down operations after crash that killed 6
item.description: The helicopter, a Bell 206 L-4, crashed into the Hudson River Thursday, killing all six people aboard.
item.dc:creator: Emily Rahhal, KRON4

item.title: Key takeaways from the Bernie Sanders and Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez rally in SLC
item.description: Thousands of Utahns filled the Jon M. Huntsman Center at the University of Utah on April 13 to listen to Sen. Bernie Sanders (I-VT) and Rep. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez (D-NY) speak as part of Sanders' "Fighting Oligarchy" tour.
item.dc:creator: Trevor Myers, ABC 4

item.title: Nicky Katt of 'Dazed and Confused,' 'Boston Public' dies: reports
item.description: Nicky Katt, who was known for his roles in "Dazed and Confused" and "Boiler Room," has died, according to multiple reports. He was 54 years old.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, WFLA, NBC

item.title: FOUND: 12-year-old girl considered endangered after running away from home
item.description: A Utah Endangered Missing Advisory has been activated after a 12-year-old girl reportedly ran away from her South Salt Lake home on Saturday.
item.dc:creator: Trevor Myers, ABC 4

item.title: 6 injured after explosion, house collapse in Austin, Texas
item.description: One house was "leveled" while another "suffered severe collapse damage" following an explosion in Austin on Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Julianna Russ, NewsNation

item.title: Popular soup, bowl products may be contaminated; USDA issues public health alert
item.description: The USDA has issued a nationwide public health alert warning consumers that some popular soup and bowl products could be contaminated with wood.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Langenfeld, WGN 9
